body:
Hey all- awesome to see the progress on this.

@XhmikosR regarding not wanting to include the SDK JS file, I think that makes sense. Fortunately there isn’t much magic happening in it, as all it’s doing under the hood is creating a form and submitting the data to our [POST API](https://developer.stackblitz.com/docs/platform/post-api) endpoint.

I think the simplest option would be to take the core logic from the SDK’s [generate.ts](https://github.com/stackblitz/core/blob/master/sdk/src/generate.ts) file and modify it for bootstrap’s purposes specifically. This is similar to what Angular.io does for their doc examples as well. This would allow you to include the functionality in your existing JS setup without a dependency on CDNs or any other external dependency.

We also have a PR open for the Firefox bug that should be landed shortly- will circle back once that’s live!

author: XhmikosR

body:
> It seems there's a `gitignore` config option we could use too, not sure if it's enabled by default and I can't figure it out from their docs.

Found this in the [v5.12.0](https://github.com/streetsidesoftware/cspell/releases/tag/v5.12.0):
```
/**
 * Tells the spell checker to load `.gitignore` files and skip files that match the globs in the `.gitignore` files found.
 * @default false
 */
useGitignore?: boolean;
```
It was specified in their [`cspell.schema.json`](https://github.com/streetsidesoftware/cspell/blob/59c75e022705319ab7eaa2fa8b77296674288e28/cspell.schema.json):
```json
"useGitignore": {
    "default": false,
    "description": "Tells the spell checker to load `.gitignore` files and skip files that match the globs in the `.gitignore` files found.",
    "type": "boolean"
},
```
In the [current source code](https://github.com/streetsidesoftware/cspell/blob/main/cspell.schema.json) it is still `false` by default.
The value could be switched in a next version. It was [supposed to be done in v5.13.0 apparently](https://github.com/streetsidesoftware/cspell/pull/1823#issuecomment-934058439). Don't know if it has been abandoned or forgotten.

author: GeoSot

body:
I've just tested 2 different approaches by disabling cache and enabling throttling (Regular 2G). The `@import` version appears to be longer to be downloaded: 14.71s. The reason is that the loading is sequential: `example.css` is loaded after `dropdown.css`.

When removing the `@import` and rather do it directly in the `dropdowns/index.html` (it will be inserted as a `<link>`):
```
extra_css:
  - "../examples.css"
  - "dropdowns.css"
```
both the CSS files are loaded at the same time and the global downloaded time of the CSS files is 8.34s.

It seems that the 2nd approach would be better for the 1st load of the `examples.css`.

With cache enabled it doesn't make any difference after the 1st load.

body:
This is interesting, and you've pinged me at a good time. I was running a slightly outdated version of chrome 96.0.4664.110, and your [draft test](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/pull/35659#issuecomment-1009760336)  in my chrome browser returns results _correctly_ as you'd expect. `false` when collapsed, `true` when open. It also returns correctly in the most up to date Safari, and Firefox browsers.

I then checked for chrome updates and found an update available to 97.0.4692.71 (the same version as the failing tests). Now I'm seeing _incorrect_ results returned from the draft test in chrome. It always returns `true`.

> So am I doing something wrong or may something else is changed on details visibility ?

Seems to me something has changed [in the latest release of chrome](https://chromereleases.googleblog.com/2022/01/stable-channel-update-for-desktop.html) ([full change log](https://chromium.googlesource.com/chromium/src/+log/96.0.4664.110..97.0.4692.71?pretty=fuller&n=10000))

It certainly feels like this is not correct behavior from the browser... but I think if necessary we could at least patch it on our end by adding something like a `if element is inside details:not([open])` check?

body:
@GeoSot - ok i'm sorry for too small amount of informations.

So... sorry for language...

Link to tests: https://codepen.io/szymon-koronkiewicz/pen/OJxaPym

We have a button. This button DON'T HAVE title. Why? Because our popover have individualy generatet content, and this content is based not on title of element but on result of function that we put as content when we initiate popover. 

We have bootstrap 5.1.3 with jquery. Code in link is not beatufiul but tell as many things about our problem.

Function that i gave as content into popover do three things. First increase by one variable named iteration. Second put actual iteration value into html code. Third is return actual date (this date should be insert into popover every time when popover opens).

Corect behavior (first click): we click on button. Iteration increase to one, one is put into html (we should see one on the bottom of button) and popover should open with actual date.
Cirect behavior (next click): we click on button. Iteration increace to two, two is put into html (we should see two on the bottom of button) and popover should open with actual (NEW) date different thant first one date (becacues some time was remain).

Actual behavior (first click): we click on button. Iteration increase to two (function was runned twice - i don't know why), two is put into html (we see two on the bottom of button), and popover open with actual date. Incorrect is that function is running twice, rest is ok.
Actual behavior (next click): we click on button. Iteration increase by one to three (correct because before we had two), three is put into html (correct), and popover open BUT not with new date, but with old date generated before.

==========

So you see? Function that i put as content is running every time when i click on button. I don't know why this function is running twice for first time. So in this situation there is no performance PLUS because function is running all the time (plus one on the begginning).

==========

!!!

Interesting is that when we insert attribute title (this is important) into button, then when we click for first time on button function insterted as content is running only one time and return actual date that is put into popover. BUT when we click again variable iteration no change and that is why we know, that function is running only one time when we first time click on the button.

Conclusion is that you can see this problem only when button dosn't have title attributte and behavior of module is almost correct because it seems to know that function must be running again and again but not using result of this function (function that is put into content of popover).

So i think that this wasn't a performance solution but regular bug.

body:
For what I tried on my side, with the previous CodePen from @blaskognia, the content is played twice when there's no title. Here are my concerns about it:

First of all, a title could be added directly to the object passed to Popover (or the attribute `title`), it'd remove the call to content in this case. But I don't think that this is the main issue in here:
* If we set a title with a function, it'll be called twice.
* For me the real problem comes from the `_isWithContent()` function itself (resp. overridden in popover). Indeed, this function calls `_getTitle()` (resp. `_getTitle() || _getContent()`) that calls itself (resp. themselves) `_resolvePossibleFunction(arg)`. So to check existence, we do the same process as if we would build it. IMO, the best way to handle it is to remove these calls. For example: `_isWithContent() { return this._getTitle() || this._config.content }` in popover file remove the second call to content.

I don't know if you want me to commit/PR on this branch @GeoSot?
author: louismaximepiton

body:
>If we set a title with a function, it'll be called twice.

because it tries to resolve it every time we call it. It was what I was trying to avoid there, but seems other devs need it

>I don't know if you want me to commit/PR on this branch @GeoSot?

You are right. Probably is better to open a pr, so we can check the differences

Regarding `aria-current`, the [ARIA design pattern](https://www.w3.org/TR/wai-aria-practices/#breadcrumb) says that if current element is not a link, `aria-current` is optional — meaning it doesn't need to be a link to match the pattern expectations.

author: thewebartisan7

body:
Thanks for the PR and your involvement!

I don't think this should be done, here's why:

- this is not consistent across variables (and even the modal component itself) since for example the next variable remains `.5rem`.
- we use `rem` for sizing, as a convention, and thus don't check for units and don't prepare to mixing units either.
- you can easily change `$modal-inner-padding` to use a `px` value, which would solve your issue and make your sizing more consistent too.

IMHO there's no need to change the default values, and I strongly recommend you either change the other variable or use `rem` across your codebase.

I'm closing this for now, but feel free to comment back here if you have anything else to share—we may reconsider this if needed.
author: ffoodd

body:
Thanks for your quick reply, appreciate it!

I noticed that the next variable was `.5rem` too, but that one compiled fine (which is a bad reason not to have changed it in my PR now that I think about it). But now that we're talking about it, shouldn't it be relative to the `$spacer` variable too?

What I've done in my PR is something I see in other places too:
[#L582](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/scss/_variables.scss#L582), [#L1072](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/scss/_variables.scss#L1072), [#L1131](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/scss/_variables.scss#L1131), [#L1219](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/scss/_variables.scss#L1219), [#L1282](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/scss/_variables.scss#L1282), [#L1448](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/scss/_variables.scss#L1448)

But it seems to be missing in other places that are about the same type of variable (`padding`s/`margin`s):
[#L1472](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/scss/_variables.scss#L1472), [#L1495](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/scss/_variables.scss#L1495), [#L1496](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/scss/_variables.scss#L1496), [#L1525](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/scss/_variables.scss#L1525) and others.

Changing the `$spacer` value will be ignored by other variables that are about `padding`s and `margin`s too.
Is there a specific reason for it, or am I misunderstanding?

One last note, which sounds conceited but I totally don't mean it in that way:
Changing `$modal-inner-padding` to a `px` value isn't going to help; it's still going to break compilation since it's being used in a calculation later with the `rem` variable `$modal-footer-margin-between`. Setting that variable to a `px` would fix it though.

Thanks again for your in-depth reply and recommendations, it helps me learn to make the most out of Bootstrap!

author: ffoodd

body:
I just stumbled upon this change by accident – after upgrading from `5.1.3` to `5.2.0`, the entire site layout shifted due to the changed behaviour of the `$container-padding-x` variable. We had this in our custom `_variables.scss`:

```scss
$container-padding-x: 1.5rem;
```

To get back to the previous layout, we had to double that value (to get the same padding as before as now the padding is halved in the `make-container()` mixin:

```scss
$container-padding-x: 3rem;
```

I couldn't find any mention of this in the release notes for 5.2.0 or in the upgrade guide. I had to manually dig through the [commit history for `scss/mixins/_container.scss`](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/commits/main/scss/mixins/_container.scss) to find out why my layout was broken. This should probably be mentioned somewhere as it's a huge change.
author: MoritzLost

body:
> To get back to the previous layout, we had to double that value What causes at least one problem. Example: ```
foo
bar
``` The background color of the columns will have a gap to the viewport if the container inline padding is larger then `calc(--bs-gutter-x * 0.5)`. If you don't have such case, this might work well for you. Otherwise it seems that there is no solution to have a different container inline padding from `calc(--bs-gutter-x * 0.5)`. Not even using flexbox gap instead the old technique will help (see https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues/35282#issuecomment-2455353677).

body:
Pushed a big update here that revamps the toggler to be a dropdown so we can accept light mode, dark mode, or automatic (based on system preference). My JS isn't the best, but it feels decent and works well lol. Would love some eyes on it.

| Light | Dark |
| --- | --- |
| ![home-light-v2](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/98681/155606279-3c81ca27-d60f-4e77-80a9-8343b2920bcf.png) | ![home-dark-v2](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/98681/155606288-52addcc3-c56d-46d7-9a51-f1979a35258e.png) |
| ![docs-light-v2](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/98681/155606289-b907f772-5398-454e-814e-13f611ced6e2.png) | ![docs-dark-v2](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/98681/155606291-b63bd795-becb-4a4e-afee-ea1df741fd47.png) |

And here's how the theme toggler looks in the navbar. Note that as you change the theme, the icon in the navbar updates accordingly to indicate what theme is active.

![home-light-v2-picker](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/98681/155606285-39cc9f32-48ef-4b44-98e0-a7be6e73fc39.png)

body:
> Do we refer to these as themes or color modes?

I think color modes fits better, since the word themes is already used.

> Do we provide a color mode toggler JS plugin?

Probably not, since there are so many ways of realizing them, from buttons to dropdowns and cookies to local storage. Beside that it seems to be very easy to implement by now.

Thank you for your work!

body:
After playing around a bit with it I am already eagerly awaiting BS 5.3. A few things however I noticed (until now, maybe more follow when using it more):

1. The dark button is not discernible as it has the same color as the background unless hovering. A tint similar to the input group elements or card headers should help here.
2. Headings inside light cards are not readable (though that might be solved after a rebase on master?). Same for the border between card body and header/footer.
3. `[data-theme="dark"]` should also set transitive variables like `--bs-form-control-bg`. These refer to other variables however setting the referred variables to a new value in a child scope does not update the other variables transitively.

body:
Hello @mdo 

I modified and fixed a few errors in the documentation. This is **just documentation**. Not components.

- Added the color scheme: light and dark. https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/color-scheme
- Added the color scheme sample to the stretched-link and gutters documentation and deleted the bg-light class.
- Deleted the bg-light class from several documentations to make the documentation nicer.

[Full diff here](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/compare/new-masthead-darkmode...czirok:bootstrap-new-masthead-darkmode:new-masthead-darkmode)

There are several *-dark color errors left, further research is needed:

~~- /docs/5.2/helpers/colored-links/~~ - Fix: https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/pull/35857#issuecomment-1236054861
~~- /docs/5.2/utilities/colors/#colors~~ - Fix: https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/pull/35857#issuecomment-1236054861
~~- /docs/5.2/components/card/#border~~ - Fix: https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/pull/35857#issuecomment-1236054861

- /docs/5.2/components/carousel/#dark-variant

~~- /docs/5.2/components/navbar/#external-content~~ - Fix: https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/pull/35857#issuecomment-1236350647
~~- /docs/5.2/components/navbar/#offcanvas~~ - Fix: https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/pull/35857#issuecomment-1236350647

**Todos and open questions: Do we refer to these as themes or color modes?**

The documentation calls this "color schemes". https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/color-scheme. I personally vote for this. I think "themes" and "color modes" words place: html>head>meta keywords for search engines.

**Some thoughts**

Dark scheme logo? /docs/5.2/about/brand/
Disabled color in dark shema maybe litle more darker?

body:
Hello everyone.

Text muted color restored. There were problems during testing.

**Navbar toggle icon - hamburger**

There was a big bug in new-masthead-darkmode. SVG does not support variables in CSS. Unfortunately.

It is impossible to change the color of SVG in CSS, with a variable. Nightmare.

I use an elegant solution for light and dark themes. Very little modification. There are two hamburgers in one SVG. A light and a dark. The dark, under the light. When changing the scheme, I change the background-position. I modified the navbar-dark class, added this solution. No need to add data-bs-theme="dark" attribute and 100% backwards compatible.

I checked the example pages and corrected small errors.

[Text muted restore. Navbar support light and dark scheme svg in css. Examples fix.](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/commit/8dbf6023ff1326b66d1c6ddb80298ba14eeec7b4)

[Good testing...](https://github.com/czirok/bootstrap-new-masthead-darkmode)

Execute `npm run dist` before `npm start`.

body:
Several components use $theme-colors.

- .table-$theme-colors
- .button-$theme-colors
- .button-outline-$theme-colors
- .alert-$theme-colors
- .list-group-item-$theme-colors
- .text-bg-$theme-colors
- .link-$theme-colors
- .border-$theme-colors
- .text-$theme-colors
- .bg-$theme-colors

With new CSS classes

- $theme-color-border 
- $theme-color-bg
- $theme-color-color
- $theme-color-link
- and|or hover, active, stb
- maybe  emphasis, muted, subtle

and associated CSS variables

- --$prefix-$theme-color-border 
- --$prefix-$theme-color-bg
- --$prefix-$theme-color-color
- --$prefix-$theme-color-link
- ...

Components can use --$prefix-$theme-color-* CSS variables. Everything would be inherited.
author: czirok

body:
Yeah, I dig :). That's essentially the goal for v6 when we can refactor some things and make some larger, breaking changes. We want to have fewer, better utilized global Sass variables that map to CSS variables that can be reused. Unsure how much we can get there in v5 without causing a lot of headaches along the way.

For example, the new selectors you added for nested themes is a step too far for v5. I don't want to go creating more complicated specificity issues and selectors. But the issue of nesting is well heard and I added some examples in the docs to help test that. It's not _perfect_, but it feels good so far.

Hello Bootstrap team!

In this project, i collected the color usage from all SCSS. I write SCSS, which generates theme CSS from background color and foreground color and Bootstrap colors.

It follows the Bootstrap rules, is very small and produces a nice result. Integrates with Bootstrap.

Contains several colors. There are "Gen Light" and "Gen Dark". This is the same color as in this project. And more colors just to test. All generated.

body: The latest changes include a sizable update to the colors: - Adds new "emphasis color" for higher contrast foreground text (not for backgrounds, at least not yet) - Adds new text colors, subtle background colors, and subtle border colors for higher contrast text, lighter background colors, and lighter border colors. These new colors are all adaptive to the color mode, so they have dark mode counterparts. Super fun to use. - Alerts and list groups have been updated to use these new variables, meaning their mixins will need to be deprecated. Still some work to do here. I wanted to include table variants in this, but the CSS for it is kind of gnarly. I've adapted the form validation styles to include this as well, but it's equally gnarly. - Light and Dark also got their new colors, which makes for some confusing language for the `$dark-text-dark` variable, but it actually kind of works. I think in v6, we'll consolidate `secondary`, `body-secondary`, `body-tertiary`, `light`, and `dark` into `fg` and `bg` classes/variables. Some additional changes: - Updates added-in/deprecated-in badges to use new classes. - Fixes the backdrop Chrome CSS bug (using a pseudo-element instead of outright removing it) I think it'd be advantageous to review a lot of this PR with @julien-deramond and @ffoodd over a Zoom/Slack call to get a better handle on it given the breadth of changes. Here's an example of the new colors:

body: I suppose a lot of bootstrap users would find it very helpful to have media-query support (light/dark theme) while using the distributed css, without having to fiddle with scss/mixins. I know I would. If opt-in is desired, it could for example work by specifying something like `<html data-bs-theme="media-query">`.
author: Bouke

body:
> If opt-in is desired, it could for example work by specifying something like `<html data-bs-theme="media-query">`.

I think an explicit opt-out would be simpler to implement. By default it would respect the users color preference and to opt-out, the developer would have to define a theme on a top level element like `<html data-bs-theme="light">` to say that they want to override the users preference.

body: Something worth noting @julien-deramond is that I get an error of `Error: Undefined mixin.` when I move the `color-mode()` mixin from `_functions.scss` to `_mixins.scss` or any file within `scss/mixins/`. Super strange, not sure why, but wanted to explain why it's there.
author: mdo

body:
> Something worth noting @julien-deramond is that I get an error of `Error: Undefined mixin.` when I move the `color-mode()` mixin from `_functions.scss` to `_mixins.scss` or any file within `scss/mixins/`. Super strange, not sure why, but wanted to explain why it's there.

Fixed it in https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/pull/35857/commits/0fde05e5ffebac3e20b92b71450cf00030b8cefd. It was because `bootstrap-grid.scss` imports `root` which uses `color-mode` so needed to import specifically `mixins/_color-mode.scss` also in `bootstrap-grid.scss`.
Feel free to revert the commit if you wanted to name the mixin differently or rather put it somewhere else.
author: julien-deramond

body:
Let’s start with that: great work @mdo! 👏

This PR being complex to review, I'll keep more general actions and questions in this comment to tackle maybe separately after the merge of this PR in a “tracking” issue. They don't need an immediate answer!
IMO, merging it will allow us to manipulate and use the dark mode while we are reviewing and developing other features and issues for the v5.3.0 and so testing it without thinking about it. Moreover, it will avoid having to maintain this branch and also folks who want to try it out right now to create issues with specific topics one by one.

Here are my thoughts and actions:

> **Warning**
> WIP

* For all components, when we click on StackBlitz edit buttons, do we need to keep the theme context? If it was on dark mode when we clicked on it, should it be on dark mode in StackBlitz?
* I think we should provide a way to have access to the examples in dark mode as well. But how do we allow a switch between themes? If so, we should have dark thumbnails as well as resources.
* Non-regression testing:
  * Go through all the documentation and check for non-regression in light mode in all states: hover, focus, etc. and with browser compatibility in mind
  * Go through all the documentation and check the rendering in dark mode in all states: hover, focus, etc.  and with browser compatibility in mind
  * Redo the same tests but with `$color-mode-type` set to `"media-query"`
* Check the contrast ratio of all components, examples and documentation for a11y
* Test in a separate project to create a fake brand with design tokens to check if it is usable easily
  * Same thing but with a brand that have dark variants on top of that; would mean 4 versions of each component (light, light with dark variant, dark, dark with dark variant)
* Double-check impacts on our `.browserslistrc` if any.
* After the alpha release
  * Add dark mode to the blog website
  * Add dark mode to the icons website
  * Add dark mode to the themes website

body:
I think the bigger issue unfortunately is that our utilities API hasn't aged well with the CSS variable additions I've done. I'm still learning all the kinks around this, but I wonder if we don't need CSS variables for our `border-color` and `border-width` utilities. Instead, those can be `property: value` rules. Saves on having to add a global or local (to `.border-{color}`) CSS variable for those.

**Edit:** For now, going to use a global variable here but I might want to re-evaluate some decisions here ahead of v5.2.0. Unclear just how much flexibility we're trying to give still with CSS vars, especially with regards to root vs local.

author: julien-deramond

body:
I've fleshed out the proper implementation for the functions/mixins. A couple of notable things:

1. Option getters get an extra `utilities-get-values($utility-name)` function. It returns the values for the utility, always as a map (hiding the fact that the values could be lists or even strings). I needed it for implementing some of the mixins and figured it could be useful to developers too.
2. Adding/removing values will turn the `values` option for that utility into a proper map, if it previously was a list or a string. I'm not sure if it's a big deal or not. Only thing I could think would break out of it is if:
   - someone already uses Bootstrap, 
   - has some code reading the `values` configuration of a utility themselves for whatever reason. 
   - have that code expect a list or string for the `values` of the property
   - start using `utilities-add-values`/`utilities-remove-values` once it's out
Feels like it's something that can be sorted out through documentation.
3. Implementation is not defensive at all: option getters return null if the any part of the path is missing, `set-options` creates new utilities if the utility name does not exist already for example. Do you think it'd be preferable to add some warning/errors to guide developers a little more? 

It's tested out using [this `sass-true` test suite](https://github.com/romaricpascal/bootstrap/blob/test-utilities-functions-mixins/scss/tests/mixins/_utilities.test.scss) (kept on a separate branch to not pollute the project awaiting a decision on the testing strategy). so hopefully as airtight as it can be.

body:
@mdo signposted me to this PR when raising a point elsewhere (#36168) about something that related to this one...

Since upgrading to 5.2.0 to take advantage of some of the specific changes the border radius for a button and a card looked different.

In my case a btn-lg class is in use for the button as it's trying to help with the accessibility to have it a little larger... before the change the difference between the card and button radius was 0.05rem so although they look the same there is actually a slight difference.

Since the change the difference between the card and button is more prominent as it's different by 0.125rem. That difference is catching my eye more easily and not looking quite right.  I couldn't see a class for altering the card radius to a large equivalent and imagine there isn't one.

I'm not really sure where else to continue discussing this as creating an issue to discuss it further didn't seem right either (or doesn't anymore at least). Happy to do that if you think best or wait for further guidance. Maybe a discussion is not worthwhile and it's just my tough luck and something I'll have to work around.

FWIW...

5.1.0 codepen - https://codepen.io/fitcwill/pen/ZExyvqM

5.2.0 codepen - https://codepen.io/fitcwill/pen/QWmgaZO

Ironically, the change I was starting to upgrade for makes the button look disabled when hovering over it and is similar to the disabled style itself.  I actually feel more strongly about raising something for that!

author: GeoSot

body:
I _think_ it's in a better shape now. Apologies for the too eager opening for review, and thanks for the comments 😄 

The switch to Jasmine introduced the need to add a `watch-css-test` script as Jasmine doesn't seem to provide a `--watch` option. Besides that, the setup is pretty much the same to the one described in the original PR for Mocha.  

The code actually calling sass-true has been extracted into its own module (`runner.js`) so it could be linted like the rest of the JavaScript of the project.

One thing to be aware of is that sass-true compiles in expanded mode by default, which clashes with the linting at time (for rules like `number-leading-zero`). There may be need for a few `stylelint-disable` in the expected output of tests, but I think it's better than configuring sass-true to its `compressed` mode as Bootstrap explicitely sets the `expanded`. It'll require caution if running `npm run css-lint-stylelint -- --fix` though.

body: I think we'll skip this, but wouldn't mind @ffoodd's opinion.
author: mdo

body:
I think it'll conflict with the grid at some point. I know `size` is meant for this, but is also less predictable than `width`- and a lot less than the grid.

Have a look at [MDN explanations for `size`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/input#attr-size) which clearly states that:
- CSS takes precedence,
- `size` unit depends on `type`,
- it's not allowed for every inputs.

So I'll close this —also according to last comment, but as usual feel free to discuss further if you have any other arguments.
author: ffoodd

body:
@ffoodd
> CSS takes precedence

can you explain it?

> size unit depends on type

when it's can create any problems?

> it's not allowed for every inputs.

no matter, it's allowed to "text" inputs(`text`, `email` and etc.). or you want change width of checkboxes?

It already used indirectly in [auto sizing](https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.1/forms/layout/#auto-sizing)
author: WinterSilence

body:
If any one uses CSS `width` on a form control (directly or through any helper or utility), `size` wouldn't apply.

`size="10"` won't set the same width depending on input type, so when defining a form layout containing several input types, it wouldn't be reliable for alignment. The grid is.

And finally `.form-control` can be applied to any type, so your suggestion may apply to any form control with the size attribute—even if the said input do not honor `size`—leading to an uncontained width input.

So yeah, that's a no-go IMO.
author: ffoodd

body:
@ffoodd 
> If any one uses CSS width on a form control (directly or through any helper or utility), size wouldn't apply.

i don't see any problems in this - user can customize inputs as he wish

> size="10" won't set the same width depending on input type, so when defining a form layout containing several input types, it wouldn't be reliable for alignment.

checbox/radio use `.form-check-input`, range not effect, date/time/datetime have fixed size. 
can you post example with this difference? i can't reproduce it clearly.
author: WinterSilence

body:
This could lead to unexpected behaviour for some users, and Bootstrap used to recommend the grid for such layouts, which is way more predictable.

I don't see us providing support for this due to the changes it'd introduce, the inconsistencies it could lead to (probably generating new issues), and the fact that it's unneeded and don't provide any clear advantage over the grid.

body:
> @julien-deramond Do you have any alternative in mind, to avoid this (using a link or something else)?

The only alternative I'm thinking of (not tested so maybe it is dumb) would be to do something like that:
- Have `site/assets/js/popover-demo.js` accessible from `https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.1/assets/js/` (and not only within `docs.min.js`).
- Still call the shortcode  with `example js_file="popover-demo.js"` but then having `data-js-file="popover-demo.js"` (and not the content of the file)
- When clicking on the button:
  - Request the JS file by concatenating `{baseUrl}/docs/5.1/assets/js/` and `popover-demo.js` (now exported in `assets/js`)
  - Read its content
  - Export it as it is already done in this PR in StackBlitz.

If it works and is considered as a better solution please keep in mind that we would have several JS files used only for StackBlitz in `assets/js`; and then CSS files as well to fix the other issues with StackBlitz. It would also mean that for each Pull Request, we would have more files in Netlify and that can causes issues with some quotas you may have (the global package of the documentation would be heavier).

Do you want me to try this solution to see if it is feasible?

Just noting that similar changes impacting the active / hover states the default values for `info` and `warning`.

| theme color | v4 bg | v5 bg | v4 bg hover | v5 bg hover | v4 contrast | v5 contrast |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `warning` | `#ffc107` | `#ffc107` | `#e0a800` | `#ffcd39` | [1.31](https://contrast-ratio.com/#%23e0a800-on-%23ffc107) | [1.09](https://contrast-ratio.com/#%23ffcd39-on-%23ffc107) |
| `info` | `#17a2b8` | `#17a2b8` | `#138496` | `#3dd5f3` | [1.44](https://contrast-ratio.com/#%23138496-on-%2317a2b8) | [1.74](https://contrast-ratio.com/#%233dd5f3-on-%2317a2b8) |

As can be seen, if the button colour is light enough, then `tint` is used, compared with the previous approach which darkened the colour.

body:
urgh, good lord, what have algolia done there...

ok, it's not completely bad, but some right clangers. the dialog lacks a `role="dialog"` and `aria-modal="true"`, the underlying page is not hidden from assistive tech (`aria-hidden="true"`), and it's still possible to escape out of the dialog and navigate through the underlying page (e.g. doing `Alt+d` on Windows to jump to the address bar, then `Tab` forward back into the page).

also, the oh-so-subtle text colour for the "No recent searches" and the keyboard shortcut/command hints is making my eyes go fuzzy...

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/895831/164539537-667e2d08-c898-4402-9a6c-a493a1ffc654.png)


is there an upstream repository where this can be fed back to them? had a quick look over the algolia site, but nothing jumped out at me directly...

I'd say it's ok to ship it as is for now, but really hoping that we'll be able to punt them into an improved direction

body: @GeoSot Is there anyway we can lint these snippets? I believe we have an issue open to suggest this, but I'm not sure of how we could accomplish it off the top of my head.
author: mdo

body:
> Is there anyway we can lint these snippets?

A few thoughts:

As mentioned in https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues/32096, [eslint-plugin-markdown](https://github.com/eslint/eslint-plugin-markdown) looks promising. The equivalent for HTMl seems to be [eslint-plugin-html](https://github.com/BenoitZugmeyer/eslint-plugin-html) but I'm not sure if this can lint code blocks or only the contents of script tags. Without trying, *these seem the best solution*.

Alternatively a regex something like:

```regex
```js((.|\n)*)```
```

should(?) match JS code blocks in markdown.

And for HTML:

```regex
<pre>((.|\n)*?)<\/pre>
```

could work with a bit of tweaking.

You could then run the matched strings through ESLint to flag issues. (See https://eslint.org/docs/developer-guide/nodejs-api#linter)

body:
For more context on why we would keep this version picker, see https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues/31980. We've had a handful of issues and more requests to better support jumping between versions of specific pages.

Across major versions, we need better linking and some updates for the previous major versions.
author: mdo

body:
> Let's keep in mind that it would be safer in the future to wait for the beginning of a major version to change the sitemap of the documentation (e.g. moving or renaming some sections). Otherwise, between minor versions, if the sitemap of the documentation changes too much, it will result in a lot of 404s.

Definitely. No major restructuring of pages here. Only thing we should have to worry about is _new_ pages, which we've accounted for with the `added` front matter.

> Don't know if it would be feasible but while I was testing this PR, one possible evolution would be to keep the anchor links as well:

I'm going to skip that for now, definitely a nice to have. Also a little tougher since the headings _can_ change between versions (e.g., `## Sass` became `## CSS` on a ton of pages). Not a huge deal because those links would still function, they just wouldn't scroll to the section.

body:
To fix it temporarily:

1. Remove your "node_modules" folder
2. Remove your "package-lock.json" file
3. Add to your "package.json" file: `
"overrides": {
    "autoprefixer": "10.4.5"
  }
`
4. Finally, run: `npm i --save-exact`



Some details:

- React version: `^18.1.0`
- Bootstrap: `^5.1.3`
- cra-template: `npx create-react-app my-app --template typescript`
- Node version: `v16.14.2`
- NPM version: `8.6.0`
- env: `macOS Monterey v12.3.1 | intel processor`


Hope it works for u!

body:
@[XhmikosR](https://github.com/XhmikosR)
If this is the case and you want to leave it in, you should use an older version of autoprefixer, otherwise the warning will be treated as an error in production

Also prevents automated deployments without finding workarounds to modify the installed version of autoprefixer
author: AdrianCurtin

body: It's not an error, it's a warning. And we can't control the tools the consumers of the package use.
author: XhmikosR

body:
@XhmikosR , I guess that its not specifically an error, but because its somewhat common to enable "treat warnings as errors" it does affect a lot of automatic deployments. It's true that you can't control what tools other users use, but you can control which tools that you use. So if you want to update the prefixer in v5, but not in v4, that's another way to get around these warnings.

https://caniuse.com/?search=print-color-adjust

Currently the only web browsers which *really* used only color-adjust appear to have been earlier builds of firefox for which 48-97, during which print-color-adjust enjoyed partial support. Other browsers do not support color-adjust or require the webkit prefix. 

So maintaining this to support old versions of firefox eschews support of that functionality in Edge, Chrome, Safari etc
author: AdrianCurtin

body:
>  It's not an error, it's a warning. And we can't control the tools the consumers of the package use.

I am using bootstrap 4 via the ruby gem, which used to be a supported/recommended path to using bootstrap 4. The bootstrap 4 ruby gem has a dependency on `autoprefixer-rails`.  So autoprefixer is not a tool I chose to use, it's in fact one bootstrap did control. 

While my current setup doesn't cause any builds to fail, I now get this autoprefixer warning in all my build output -- written directly to stdout, in the middle of my build output.  Which is a bit annoying. 

I don't think I have a way to remove autoprefixer from my app, as it's a bootstrap dependency; I guess I could lock to an older version that doesn't produce this warning.  And hope i don't lose anything else from future autoprefixers that I might need. 

If anyone has any other ideas for eliminating this warning from my use of bootstrap 4 via ruby gem, I'd be interested in hearing them.  I wish I had a way to configure autoprefixer to not output this warning, but I don't believe I do.

Just a question with the 2 last examples in the [badges](https://deploy-preview-36309--twbs-bootstrap.netlify.app/docs/5.3/examples/badges/). I began the creation of a [chip component](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/pull/37846) as a draft but are we saying with these examples that finally a badge and a chip are the same thing? I don't have a strong opinion on it but if we end up creating chips, the border between what's a chip/what's a badge would be very thin 😄 

FYI at Orange, we have a separate component because the design/rendering/usage is very different. And TBH, our first version of what we call Tags (so Chips) was using `@extend .badge` in CSS.

Yeah, it's going to be a little messy with stuff like this. These snippets don't or shouldn't represent any kind of official component thinking though. This is just showing how you can take Bootstrap's defaults and do some fun and interesting things, and to help folks move a little faster.

body: What's requiring us to remove those examples using `title` if they work as expected in the docs?
author: mdo

body:
> What's requiring us to remove those examples using `title` if they work as expected in the docs?

Considering [Popovers - Live demo](https://deploy-preview-36352--twbs-bootstrap.netlify.app/docs/5.2/components/popovers/#live-demo).

In the Markdown, it is declared this way:
```md
{{< example >}}
<button type="button" class="btn btn-lg btn-danger" data-bs-toggle="popover" title="Popover title" data-bs-content="And here's some amazing content. It's very engaging. Right?">Click to toggle popover</button>
{{< /example >}}
```
At the load of this page Bootstrap JS will "consume" and delete this `title` to create the popover and display it.
As a result we have in the DOM:
```html
<button type="button" class="btn btn-lg btn-danger" data-bs-toggle="popover" data-bs-content="And here's some amazing content. It's very engaging. Right?">Click to toggle popover</button>
```
This is this code that will be used by StackBlitz when we click on the "Edit on StackBlitz" button. The title information is lost in the way so the example won't work; at least it won't work entirely as you can see when you click on it (the popover doesn't have any header/title).

Based on this observation and the [discussion we had with GeoSot](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/pull/36127#discussion_r851609188) we could hide the "Edit on StackBlitz button" for those examples. If we don't mind modifying the documentation a bit we could rather use in some of them `data-bs-title` instead of `title`.

---

_In this PR if you don't mind I would propose to not to tackle this issue in order to move on this StackBlitz topic step-by-step:_
- _Merge this PR when it is considered as good (or [the alternative one](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/pull/36127))_
- _Do the same work in another PR to handle the specific CSS code used by some examples_
- _Tackle the remaining edge cases one by one in separate PRs like the `title` for popovers and tooltips to finish all the tasks listed in https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues/36391_

author: GeoSot

body:
The main issue is if you have an external library like cash-dom that is handling the same event as Bootstrap (e.g. a click handler on a Tab) and is setting the `delegateTarget` (in this case, but I can imagine other properties with the same issue). Cash-dom makes the `delegateTarget` property read-only and Bootstrap can't just do `event.delegateTarget = something` assignment.

The second part of the patch (`configurable: true`) makes sure that read-only properties set by Bootstrap can be re-set outside.

Now thinking about this, and considering https://github.com/jquery/jquery/blob/5d5ea015114092c157311c4948f7cc3d8c8e7f8a/src/event.js#L306 we might indeed need the change you suggested. I mean it would be nice to be consistent inside Bootstrap and make all event properties read-only, but it may break compatibility with jQuery. I didn't test that.

I'm not sure to understand the modification proposed in this PR.
`$headings-color` is set to `null` by default.
Then `$headings-color` is used to define `--bs-heading-color`:
https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/78c0ad8044959dae5239eac1a6cb9a752878e88b/scss/_root.scss#L67
This CSS variable is used for the headers and the popovers headers.

If you override the value of `$headings-color` or modify `--bs-heading-color` (on the fly in your browser for example), both the headers and popovers headers will have the new color.

Am I missing something here?

body:
Have a look at https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/pull/35857 to see what I'm doing with the new secondary and tertiary bg/color options. I'm trying to make theming easier with defined, semantic/purposeful colors that are used throughout as a clearer set of rules. Little more like Apple's HIG or Google's Material.

The relevant bits from the dark mode PR are that the `.img-thumbnail` uses a global `border-color` and the `<body>`'s `background-color`. The `.figure-caption` and `.blockquote-footer` would also use the secondary color (`--bs-secondary-color`). The backdrop is the only thing that needs a little more thought yet.

The intention here is that, in future versions, fewer CSS variables (or Sass variables) need to be overridden to re-style the entire design.

body:
just in terms of regular, bare-bones styles (in Chrome and Firefox on Windows at least), `readonly` inputs look initially exactly the same as editable ones.

![screenshot showing regular, readonly, and disabled text inputs in chrome; the first two look the same](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/895831/172053051-3528edd6-0e65-409d-90c3-077957d3cce5.png)

As it's already tricky enough to try and find styles for disabled controls that don't end up with absolutely abysmal contrast, I think it'll be difficult to find some intermediate "looks a bit disabled, but not fully" look that won't become confusing. long way of saying: i'd say leave `readonly` styled as if it were a regular editable input.
author: patrickhlauke

body:
Agree that read-only inputs shouldn't be displayed like disabled inputs because it isn't the same status.
But honestly I didn't know that browsers styled read-only inputs like regular inputs. I'm more used to see in editable panels something like what's described in https://vaadin.com/docs/v8/framework/articles/ReadOnlyVsDisabledFields:

![Screenshot from 2022-06-05 20-17-33](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/17381666/172064753-bf44fea3-9163-4a97-92d1-e2bd4de10683.png)

![Screenshot from 2022-06-05 20-18-18](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/17381666/172064762-daeb5680-e8ed-401e-bb46-bffbad0e7fa0.png)

As an end-user, if you ask me to fill a form with a read-only input looking exactly like a regular input, I'd probably think instinctively that the form is buggy.

The link I've provided to show what I thought to be a "normal" design thing is an old version of Vaadin. In the current version of Vaadin, it's interesting to see how they display a read-only input: https://vaadin.com/docs/latest/ds/components/input-fields/#read-only:

![Screenshot from 2022-06-05 20-22-03](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/17381666/172064870-df753125-22de-4ab2-ba94-b12271727d65.png)

This subtle difference with dotted border could help the end-user understanding this is read-only and not editable.
author: julien-deramond

body:
maybe we provide an in-between style for `:readonly` where the outer border is much less contrasty, but otherwise same as for editable inputs (i.e. leave background colour and text colour alone).

@julien-deramond fun aside: in my day job, i did an accessibility audit for vaadin about a year or so ago and worked with them on some improvements - can't remember if disabled vs readonly fields were part of that though ... should go and revisit some of my advice at the time
author: patrickhlauke

body: @julien-deramond https://vaadin.com/blog/upcoming-accessibility-improvements
author: patrickhlauke

body:
I agree with having just the outer border be differentiated for readonly inputs. Less contrasty or dotted/dashed both would work. I think dotted/dashed would make it more obvious, but at the same time prefer the solid border for its consistency.

I suppose there's no reason we couldn't do both, and then if a designer prefers solid they could just change the border style instead of having to change both that and the color of it.

body: Any fallback like that presents the same issue unfortunately—links with `.h1` would appear dark gray instead of Bootstrap blue as it'd be inheriting from the body.
author: mdo

body: @ffoodd @julien-deramond Thoughts on maybe adding a new `--bs-heading-link-color` and adding some new `a.h1, a.h2, etc` rules? As of right now, any fallback keyword value like `inherit`, `currentcolor`, `initial`, etc would result in the same behavior. The intent is to preserve the blue link colors on heading utilities (and not, fwiw, on children elements like `h1 a` to be clear). This might still then be a breaking change, but it preserves a new CSS variable and adds even more customization later (e.g., the ability to make heading links custom from body paragraph links which lots of sites do).
author: mdo

body:
Usually not in favor of using element and class selectors together, but not sure to have a better solution.

There're at least two ways I can think of:
- using [the `:any-link` pseudo-class](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/:any-link) which would feel a bit more elegant than element selector—but wouldn't differ that much;
- using a custom cascade based on custom properties, e.g. `var(--bs-link-color, var(--bs-heading-color))` which would ensure the first get applied (props to Miriam Suzanne for the technique).

I can't find any other way around for now. Any thought?
author: ffoodd

body: Hmm... for the second option, both values are always set (globally), so not sure a custom cascade would help much?
author: mdo

body:
Uh, you're right. I don't think it's safe to move `--bs-link-color` to links reboot, sadly, since it could be used on other elements than links.

I can't find any other way. Let's go for `a.hn` then?

author: GeoSot

body:
> Thank you a lot @Sentox6 Maybe is duplicated of #36401?

Definitely addressing the same issue; however, perhaps one drawback of only using a mouse-down event is that the user cannot avoid dismissing the modal by moving the mouse back inside the dialog before releasing their click. It may create the reverse issue where (for example) the user tries to select some text in the modal, and if their click starts just outside, it will dismiss immediately.

I generally prefer behaviour that doesn't trigger until mouseup, too, but that's just my opinion.

thank you @louismaximepiton for your consistency and for opening again the PR.

May I ask one question? The change on line 347 & 448 depends on the other two?

Why, I am asking:
https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/c1813ef2bc2a398e5a9bcdd398608f8a021574e3/js/src/tooltip.js#L376-L378
`_getTitle` is used to resolve this duplication, and in advance, it uses the `_resolvePossibleFunction` to resolve possible given function. And of course `function` is not null but maybe returns an empty string or null

So maybe we can initialize `title` & `content` in the end of ` _configAfterMerge(config) {`
after the checks and check after the initialized properties? ?
https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/c1813ef2bc2a398e5a9bcdd398608f8a021574e3/js/src/tooltip.js#L600-L605
author: GeoSot

body:
> thank you @louismaximepiton for your consistency and for opening again the PR.

Sorry to re-open with so much delay 😖 

> May I ask one question? The change on line 347 & 448 depends on the other two?

No, it's an independent change for me. This is done to reduce the bundle but I might be wrong. The 2 same lines are called in `show()` and I couldn't spot any use of `this.tip` between the actual removal and the removal in `show()`. So this way, `this.tip` is still removed but if it is there for the comprehension of the code it might be kept here, 
 
> Why, I am asking:
> 
> https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/c1813ef2bc2a398e5a9bcdd398608f8a021574e3/js/src/tooltip.js#L376-L378
> 
> `_getTitle` is used to resolve this duplication, and in advance, it uses the `_resolvePossibleFunction` to resolve possible given function. And of course `function` is not null but maybe returns an empty string or null
> 
> So maybe we can initialize `title` & `content` in the end of ` _configAfterMerge(config) {` after the checks and check after the initialized properties? ?

I think I might have missed something here. You wanna try something like: `config.title = this._getTitle()` (or equivalent) to run the function only once per tip declaration and remove the other calls to `getTitle`?
So the function would be run once, put its result and we only need to check for existence? I have few more questions:

* Do we need to change the `_getContentForTemplate()` function ?
* Since it will be called with `_getConfig()` only, that is itself called by the constructor only, if the function send a dynamic result, would it be overridden by each time it recreates ? 

---

Thinking about that, I don't know if this was your idea, but if with actual `_isWithContent()` implementation, we do something like:

```js
this._config.title = this._getTitle()
if (!(this._isWithContent() && this._isEnabled)) { // l.197
  return
}
```

Then we might need to change `_getContentForTemplate()` to use `this._config.title` instead of `this._getTitle()`. I'll give it a try in the next commit to see if it could work.
author: louismaximepiton

body:
> Do we need to change the _getContentForTemplate() function ?

Probably not


-----



> Since it will be called with _getConfig() only, that is itself called by the constructor only, if the function send a dynamic result, would it be overridden by each time it recreates ?

And now we are getting closer to the real problem:
Options:

1.   resolve possible dynamic results on the constructor (`getConfig`)
        -  pros : calls to dynamic calls are done only once, save calculation time
        - cons : possible dynamic calls are not updated 
2.  resolve possible dynamic results every time we need them(`getConfig`)
         -  pros : possible dynamic calls are always updated
         - cons : calls to dynamic calls are done and checked every time

So in each case you are facing a different issue, where NOBODY can be pleased . The question here is what is the best of the two available solutions. 
I had picked the first, not knowing that there ware cases, devs made changes and wanted the tooltip to be updated automatically

After an issue, I was forced to return to the second option. But if we do not resolve in each check our possibly dynamic calls we will be never sure about their real result
author: GeoSot

body:
> So in each case you are facing a different issue, where NOBODY can be pleased . The question here is what is the best of the two available solutions. I had picked the first, not knowing that there ware cases, devs made changes and wanted the tooltip to be updated automatically
> 
> After an issue, I was forced to return to the second option. But if we do not resolve in each check our possibly dynamic calls we will be never sure about their real result

It took a long time for me but I think I finally get to your point (I guess 😅) I wonder if it exists a simple solution like: compute title once per rebuild, store the value and use it where it's needed in order to not compute the potential functions several times (as it is done in main). From what I've seen, it would introduce more complexity to the component and maybe increase bundle size. Do you want me to give a last try for a working sample on what I've in mind? Or should it be kept for `v5.3.0+`?
author: louismaximepiton

body:
It wouldn't introduce more complexity, but we would fallback to the previous version, with a cached value (just the case I 'solved' in the previous PR). 😔
So for now I suggest just try the one change u proposed on 'setContent' and not something more . (After the release of course you may try any improvement 😉)

body:
Looking good, and correctly addresses the issue https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues/36596

At a higher level though, I'm wondering if we should just always enable focus trapping? Or, if we *do* want to allow non-modal offcanvases, we should document this clearly in the docs (currently it seems the docs are silent about focus behaviour at all) and also make sure that `aria-modal="true"` is only added when it's intended to be a modal offcanvas
author: patrickhlauke

body:
>  At a higher level though, I'm wondering if we should just always enable focus trapping? Or, if we do want to allow non-modal offcanvases, we should document this clearly in the docs (currently it seems the docs are silent about focus behaviour at all) and also make sure that aria-modal="true" is only added when it's intended to be a modal offcanvas


Either way, an offcanvas that doesn't scroll or has a backdrop, behaves like modal, so I think is right for know.
BUT yes, we may, can discuss it for next releases

body:
> @mdo I don't think I should be able to update the .bundlewatch.config file. Right?

Given the content of this PR, you can update the following corresponding values of `.bundlewatch.config.json` by adding a step of 0.25:

```
FAIL ./dist/css/bootstrap-utilities.css: 8.39KB > 8.25KB (gzip)
FAIL ./dist/css/bootstrap-utilities.min.css: 7.68KB > 7.5KB (gzip)
FAIL ./dist/css/bootstrap.css: 29.44KB > 29.25KB (gzip)
FAIL ./dist/css/bootstrap.min.css: 27.33KB > 27.25KB (gzip)
```

So for example the modification for `bootstrap-utilities.css` will be:
```diff
diff --git a/.bundlewatch.config.json b/.bundlewatch.config.json
index cea957f5e..0fda9d5d6 100644
--- a/.bundlewatch.config.json
+++ b/.bundlewatch.config.json
@@ -18,7 +18,7 @@
     },
     {
       "path": "./dist/css/bootstrap-utilities.css",
-      "maxSize": "8.25 kB"
+      "maxSize": "8.5 kB"
     },
     {
       "path": "./dist/css/bootstrap-utilities.min.css",
```

body:
You may do a test on your code using this script `https://deploy-preview-36914--twbs-bootstrap.netlify.app/docs/5.2/dist/js/bootstrap.bundle.min.js` and feedback me. I am not so sure that you can use `title` as selector. Especially if you clone an already initialized instance. 

Note: the only sure is that can be reverted just letting `title` empty, but it is not a valid way to leave the attribute empty

author: julien-deramond

body:
Of course not mandatory for this PR: I just tried quickly but what would you think about adding some automation here with `eslint-plugin-html`?

Configuration would look like something like this:
```diff
diff --git a/.eslintignore b/.eslintignore
index a18b03a5d..04bae1541 100644
--- a/.eslintignore
+++ b/.eslintignore
@@ -3,4 +3,6 @@
 **/vendor/
 /_site/
 /js/coverage/
+/js/tests/integration/
 /site/static/sw.js
+/site/layouts/
diff --git a/js/tests/unit/.eslintrc.json b/js/tests/unit/.eslintrc.json
index 6362a1acf..6508fc062 100644
--- a/js/tests/unit/.eslintrc.json
+++ b/js/tests/unit/.eslintrc.json
@@ -1,4 +1,7 @@
 {
+  "plugins": [
+    "html"
+  ],
   "extends": [
     "../../../.eslintrc.json"
   ],
diff --git a/package.json b/package.json
index 50d4b35db..4db97cff1 100644
--- a/package.json
+++ b/package.json
@@ -63,7 +63,7 @@
     "js-compile-standalone-esm": "rollup --environment ESM:true,BUNDLE:false --config build/rollup.config.js --sourcemap",
     "js-compile-bundle": "rollup --environment BUNDLE:true --config build/rollup.config.js --sourcemap",
     "js-compile-plugins": "node build/build-plugins.js",
-    "js-lint": "eslint --cache --cache-location .cache/.eslintcache --report-unused-disable-directives .",
+    "js-lint": "eslint --cache --cache-location .cache/.eslintcache --report-unused-disable-directives --ext .html,.js .",
     "js-minify": "npm-run-all --aggregate-output --parallel js-minify-*",
     "js-minify-standalone": "terser --compress passes=2 --mangle --comments \"/^!/\" --source-map \"content=dist/js/bootstrap.js.map,includeSources,url=bootstrap.min.js.map\" --output dist/js/bootstrap.min.js dist/js/bootstrap.js",
     "js-minify-standalone-esm": "terser --compress passes=2 --mangle --comments \"/^!/\" --source-map \"content=dist/js/bootstrap.esm.js.map,includeSources,url=bootstrap.esm.min.js.map\" --output dist/js/bootstrap.esm.min.js dist/js/bootstrap.esm.js",
@@ -118,6 +118,7 @@
     "cross-env": "^7.0.3",
     "eslint": "^8.23.1",
     "eslint-config-xo": "^0.42.0",
+    "eslint-plugin-html": "^7.1.0",
     "eslint-plugin-import": "^2.26.0",
     "eslint-plugin-markdown": "^3.0.0",
     "eslint-plugin-unicorn": "^42.0.0",
```

And a new `js/tests/visual/.eslintrc.json`:
```json
{
  "plugins": [
    "html"
  ],
  "extends": "../../../.eslintrc.json",
  "parserOptions": {
    "sourceType": "module"
  },
  "settings": {
    "html/html-extensions": [".html"]
  }
}
```

body: You're going to need to include a live example of the problem, please. Right now we explicitly don't support mixed validation styles as that sounds like a recipe for disaster on our part, but I'm open to making improvements wherever we can if they're sensible and don't break other things. For example, this could break existing selectors folks are using.
author: mdo

body: Closing until we hear more from the above comment.
author: mdo

body: Sorry for super late answer. Thank you for response. It happens when we mix in-browser validation with backend validation. We cannot go without server-side validation while in-browser validation is a good complement for it. Please, take a look at this CodePen: https://codepen.io/desofto/pen/MWXwOQw

body: @mdo Please check CodePen using link above and fill in the form with any name and email to see the bug - email field is marked in green as valid while should be invalid because of `is-invalid`

body: This PR sounds good to me if I understand it right. I get the urge for having less clever JS, but a code snippet we include in our docs no longer works after an update, so I think we should continue to support it without extra work. It's maybe less idea for us, but it keeps the developer experience better for folks building with Bootstrap :).

body:
In our team we don't use button-like checks and radios (even though sometimes we really want to) for the reasons you mentioned. This would be a very valued fix for us.

Quick question: Is it intentional to keep the outline (with mouse use / click) on elements like `.form-check` (checkbox, radio, switch)? I would have thought that `.form-check` and button-like checks and radios would behave the same way.
author: florianlacreuse

body:
> Quick question: Is it intentional to keep the outline (with mouse use / click) on elements like `.form-check` (checkbox, radio, switch)? I would have thought that `.form-check` and button-like checks and radios would behave the same way.

@florianlacreuse yeah that's probably something we'll want to revisit as well. admittedly, for this PR, I concentrated solely on the button-like checks/radios which have been problematic for ages. once I get a moment I'll look at all the other places where `:focus-visible` is probably more appropriate, now that it's mostly supported in browsers

> Few fixes in tables.

> This leads me to other questions:
> 
> * Why do we have [`Tab`](https://twbs-bootstrap.netlify.app/docs/5.2/components/navs-tabs/#javascript-behavior) javascript documentation inside [`List-Group`](https://twbs-bootstrap.netlify.app/docs/5.2/components/list-group/#javascript-behavior)? Seems like it was just a copy-paste.
> * Why the [`Tab`](https://twbs-bootstrap.netlify.app/docs/5.2/components/navs-tabs/#methods) javascript methods aren't in a `<table>`?

author: MadhavanBS

body:
> * Why do we have [`Tab`](https://twbs-bootstrap.netlify.app/docs/5.2/components/navs-tabs/#javascript-behavior) javascript documentation inside [`List-Group`](https://twbs-bootstrap.netlify.app/docs/5.2/components/list-group/#javascript-behavior)? Seems like it was just a copy-paste.

I think just to indicate that list groups can be used with the Tab plugin. I think in v6, maybe v5.x, we could split the Navs & Tabs page into separate pages. Tabs is just about JS functionality and works with any of our navs and list groups as far as I know. I think there was a PR to add support for arbitrary elements but I can't recall. @GeoSot might remember.

> * Why the [`Tab`](https://twbs-bootstrap.netlify.app/docs/5.2/components/navs-tabs/#methods) javascript methods aren't in a `<table>`?

They definitely should be! Maybe open an issue to track, or tackle in a PR if you like :).

On first view, I was ready to approve, but I got in seconds thoughts. Short answer, better stay as already is :
**Explanation:** According to https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/transition-duration, is valid to have the value `transition-duration: 3s, 1s;` So I changed offcanvas css to : ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/22406063/190258706-62b9eec2-ce6a-4767-b578-944cdc05c02b.png) Built the assets and added some hard-way debug helpers ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/22406063/190258878-dd5225be-0515-4a95-87a4-c7b3d37c40d1.png) Browsed to my local documentation offcanvases and opened one.... The results are not so encouraging ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/22406063/190259129-1dd42d33-e956-4a61-838b-6c826cb6f855.png) Unfortunately, it can bee seen that the results without the `parseFloat`, may be strings, and on my as it depends on me, is better not to mess js mathematical calculations, involving strings However, @yyjiugui your effort is much appreciated, so please do not hesitate to suggest future improvements
author: GeoSot

body: Hello, I would like to ask, how can I fully test the code after I change the code to ensure that it will not affect other modules.
author: yyjiugui

body: Yeah, this is intentional for the aforementioned reason.
author: XhmikosR

body:
> Hello, I would like to ask, how can I fully test the code after I change the code to ensure that it will not affect other modules.

A nice question. :love_you_gesture: 

For this scenario, I suppose we would need to `index.spec.js` and add a new testCase.
The testCase should contain an element (to the `fixture.innerHtml`), having given style with a like `transition-duration: 0.3s, 1s;` .
After this, we would need to call `getTransitionDurationFromElement` using the HTMLelement we created, and to test that the result will be a float value (0.3)

We would be glad to accept a PR containing this test
author: GeoSot

Looking back the commits [history](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/commits/main/js/src/tab.js), 
it seems that Tab was handling only the removal of active class 
https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/63d38b19740eac25b8603dc9a9ae11e1df4667f7/js/src/tab.js#L116-L120

So if we want to bring back the exact old behavior, I think it would be better to rename the `const SELECTOR_DROPDOWN_ITEM`, to

@cpsievert what do you think about this?

confirming that when I check https://deploy-preview-37246--twbs-bootstrap.netlify.app/docs/5.2/components/toasts/ and trigger the live toast example, it can be dismissed with `Esc`.

worth adding extra prose to the docs that explains this behaviour, and the short descriptions on the tests could probably do with a slight language tweak to be a bit more readable.

worth also testing how this behaves in more complex situations: a toast triggered from inside a modal dialog for instance - does the `Esc` just close the toast, or the modal as well? if it just closes the toast, and the modal is left as it was, is a subsequent `Esc` press then correctly closing the modal? is the event listener for `Esc` removed if the toast automatically disappears?

as a side note, we probably want a similar behaviour for popovers (so they can be dismissed)

author: mdo

body:
> > I get why we should do this one, but helpers aren't utilities and this Sass variable is primarily for utilities. Thoughts?
> 
> Atm it is done like this in __color-bg.scss_ as well. But you're right. Since the target release is the v5.3.0, we could create a `$enable-important-helpers` to use there and explain in the migration guide or somewhere else that something has changed for `.text-bg-*`. What do you think of this proposal?

Maybe the time is now? The default behavior of !important for link colors breaks many use cases where the default classes are overridden in some custom theme. Unfortunately setting $enable-important-utilities to false affects other behavior that we don't want. Being able to turn this feature off only for colored links would be a plus.

author: leskam

body:
Hi there, tried some things on this last week, here are my concerns about a different implementation:

- For a carousel of 2 declared items, if I want to display 3, it's not feasible without automated duplication IMO (e.g. `[1|2|1]` better than `[1|2|]`)
- I tried something based on an array of id (e.g. `[5,6,1]`). Using `order` helped to have a great visual rendering but I feel like the focus order is awful, so I think this solution should be dropped.
- I tried something based on relocating the items depending on what needs  to be displayed but it would break the existing for what I tried. I maybe missed some things but I'd drop this solution too.


On another topic,
do you think something like `data-bs-items="1,2,3,4,5,6"` could do the trick for the breakpoints (each number represent the number of items that should be displayed on `xs,sm,md,lg,xl,xxl`) ?
author: louismaximepiton

body:
> On another topic,
> do you think something like `data-bs-items="1,2,3,4,5,6"` could do the trick for the breakpoints (each number represent the number of items that should be displayed on `xs,sm,md,lg,xl,xxl`) ?

I think for better consistency, configuration should use JSON values like [explained in Carousel DOCS](https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.2/components/carousel/#options), example:
`data-bs-config="{"items-breakpoints":{"sm":1, "lg": 3}}"`

I would also change the name `data-bs-items` to `data-bs-multiple` or `data-bs-multiple-items` (boolean) for better clarity.


> * For a carousel of 2 declared items, if I want to display 3, it's not feasible without automated duplication IMO (e.g. `[1|2|1]` better than `[1|2|]`)

In this situation it should only shows 2 items because they are in range (3) - the fact that an item is missing is a fault of developer
author: leskam

body:
As a personal preference, I would suggest keeping the code as much as agnostic can be, with less complexity, as the maintenance, can be a real pain


> On another topic, do you think something like `data-bs-items="1,2,3,4,5,6"` could do the trick for the breakpoints (each number represent the number of items that should be displayed on `xs,sm,md,lg,xl,xxl`) ?

Let let the dev decide the breakpoints, ex: bs-items=2  or bs-items=2,768|3,1200 etc (pick a pattern it suits and can be adapted from other components too on v6)


> * For a carousel of 2 declared items, if I want to display 3, it's not feasible without automated duplication IMO (e.g. `[1|2|1]` better than `[1|2|]`)

In this case, you should reduce the items programmatically, throw an exception or copy them. Again I would pick the simplest, to reduce items.

body:
instead of all this noodling with `@media (hover: ...)` (incidentally, should use `@media (any-hover: ...)` anyway - see https://css-tricks.com/interaction-media-features-and-their-potential-for-incorrect-assumptions/) , how about just removing the explicit setting of the dotted underline, leave everything else as is, and let browsers decide if they want to show the dotted underline or not? if i'm not mistaken, some mobile browsers (notably Safari/iOS and any iOS/iPad browsers at the moment) already omit the default dotted underline for `<abbr>` and `<acronym>` from their default browser styles.

i.e. keep the cursor definition and the `text-decoration-skip-ink`, just remove the `text-decoration: underline dotted`

otherwise, explicitly suppressing the underline if `any-hover: none` seems a shade opinionated to me for a reset
author: patrickhlauke

body:
Just made some quick tests (I'll check more precisely later) without Bootstrap overriding the `abbr[title]` (so the default behavior). Indeed, this behavior is better by default for Safari/iOS and iOS/iPad which don't display the dotted underline. That's not the case for Android which always display it.

Anyway, thanks a lot for you feedback. I'll re-read all of it and the link you provided and try to improve this PR :)

body: Happy to answer any question about the code 😄 One of the things buried in the previous PRs (sorry for the mess with my branches) is that the code is checked by [a test suite](https://github.com/romaricpascal/bootstrap/blob/test-utilities-functions-mixins/scss/tests/mixins/_utilities.test.scss?rgh-link-date=2022-03-13T17%3A04%3A09Z) (kept on a separate branch as it'd rely on [sass-true](https://www.npmjs.com/package/sass-true) to have been brought into the project, which is getting sorted in #36029).
author: romaricpascal

body:
@louismaximepiton I've tidied up the code in the functions and mixins so the variable names are more consistent and removed code duplication. 

I've also brought in the tests that I had used when writing the code to make sure things stay shipshape. To make the test files not too large, I've split the code for manipulating the utilities map in its own file. I think it's fine for it to have a mix of Sass functions and mixins despite being in the `mixin` folder. It's something that already happens for the breakpoints and border radius and makes it more convenient to import.
author: romaricpascal

body:
Updated again, hopefully I didn't miss anything. Thanks for the comments 😊 

> For the API page, I think we should at least keep one example of the previous method to show how that it's still feasible.

🤔 Ultimately we'd want to encourage people to use those mixins, not tamper with the `$utilities` map directly like before, so I'd lean towards not advertising that they can still `map-merge()`. We could link the "These replace our previous guidance of using Sass's built-in `map-merge()` function" from the callout (if we keep it) to the 5.2 documentation, as a little reminder (which wouldn't hurt in any case I think)

> Some few questions remains for me, but the work done since last time I reviewed it is insane, well done !

Thanks, though that was mostly bringing the tests that were already waiting in a separate branch for the [sass-true PR](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/pull/36029) to be merged, so not as big an edit as the diff makes it look like 😆

body:
About wheter the Pinned-Dependencies has a big weight in the current score, I've made some calculations:

“Critical” risk checks are weighted at 10
“High” risk checks are weighted at 7.5
“Medium” risk checks are weighted at 5
“Low” risk checks are weighted at 2.5
This we would have:

( 10 * (Dangerous-Workflow) + 7.5 * (Binary-Artifacts + Branch-Protection + Code-Review + Dependency-Update-Tool + Maintained + Signed-Releases + Token-Permissions + Vulnerabilities) + 5 * (Fuzzing + Pinned-Dependencies + <s>Packaging</s> + SAST + Security-Policy) + 2.5 * (CI-Tests + CII-Best-Practices + Contributors + License) ) / (10 + 60 + 20 + 10)

Then, considering the current score:

$$ (100 + 7.5*(10 + 8 + 9 + 10 + 10 + 0 + 0 + 10) + 60 + 75) + 5 * (0 + 7 + 10 + 10) + 2.5 * (10 + 2 + 10 + 10)) / 100 $$

$$ (100 + 7.5 *  57 + 5 * 27 + 2.5 * 32 ) /100 = 7.4 $$

Even if the Pinned Dependencies was a 10, the score would only go to 7.6.

According this, the best approach would be:

1. Signed-Releases
2. Token-Permissions (Quick-win)
3. Fuzzing
4. CII-Best-Practices

Working only to get this four to 10 would increase the score to 9.6

body:
the dropdowns with `.nav-link` are currently stylistically problematic, yes.

taking https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.2/components/navbar/#supported-content as an example, changing the control to `<button class="btn btn-link nav-link dropdown-toggle">...</button>` gets us close, but the vertical text alignment is off

![screenshot of the navbar, with the dropdown using a button, with the text in the button not aligning with the other links](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/895831/200114627-bcd3f089-2a8d-4cbe-aa81-7ca57413ce1e.png)

*that* is, i think, one of the main reasons why for some components/patterns, authors still use an `<a href="..." role="button">` at the moment...because the alternative just doesn't work out of the box.

I think that's something to consider for v6 ... reviewing all the styles to make it possible to seamlessly mix and match links and real `<button>` elements and have them line up vertically correctly

EDIT: filed https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues/37433
author: patrickhlauke

body:
First attempt, simply increasing the shading/tinting for `.active`. While the current change works well for the regular primary colour button...

![normal and active/toggled button, the latter with a much stronger background color](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/895831/200169611-6acfc4b8-7a16-437b-a722-1e9409ee0d37.png)


...the difference is not sufficient for other colours (to see them all in action, check the button dropdowns at the end of https://deploy-preview-37434--twbs-bootstrap.netlify.app/docs/5.2/components/dropdowns/#single-button)

Suspect we may need to think beyond a change in background/border, to something more drastic. Some initial ideas:

* split out the intent: keep `.active` as it is, but then make specific extra styles (either as a new class, or with more specific selectors) for toggle buttons, dropdown toggles, custom checkboxes/radio buttons...anywhere where we currently just lazily piggy-back on the `.active` class to visually show something more than just the classic `:active` state
* for dropdowns, we can make use of the down-arrow chevron icon better. have the icon point up when toggle/pressed. this provides a visual cue about the control's state that does not rely on colour
* for checkboxes/radio buttons, perhaps take the same approach as the outlined checks and radios https://deploy-preview-37434--twbs-bootstrap.netlify.app/docs/5.2/forms/checks-radios/#outlined-styles ... these don't rely specifically on use of color, as they essentially invert the control (though need to see how they fare with the lighter colours in our palette...those may need a further visual boost). for the non-outlined version, we could reverse the styles: non-checked/selected has solid background, selected/checked only has border. alternatively, for the solid background button styles, introduce an additional outline for the checked/pressed state.
* for toggle buttons, take a similar approach? go for solid vs border only? or have additional border/outline?
